metadata.title: Signal Processing with Wide Figures, Equations, and Tables
metadata.abstract: This example demonstrates the full range of IEEE layout features: a wide (full-width) figure spanning both columns, a display equation, a single-column table, and a wide table. It is intended as a reference for how the JSON document model expresses each of these block types and how the compiler positions them using continuous section breaks.

blocks.text: Figure 1 below spans both columns to show a comparison of two periodic signals over time.

blocks.caption: Comparison of sine and cosine signal amplitudes over time.

blocks.text: The relationship between the signals is captured by the following identity, shown in Equation 1.
Table I summarizes the sampling parameters used to generate the comparison figure.
Table II reports results across a wider set of configurations and is rendered as a full-width table because it has more columns than fit in a single column.

blocks.caption: Comparison of configurations across all tested scenarios

blocks.text: This example paper compiles a mix of wide and single-column elements, demonstrating the continuous section-break mechanism used for wide content.

references.title: A Framework for Deterministic Document Layout
references.venue: Proc. Example Conf. on Document Engineering
